meaning: n.(基督教)上帝一位论,集权制
examples: Although his formal break with Anglicanism came in 1782, by 1776 the shift to Unitarianism had already taken place.
I hear of Yan Cong from an interesting loose group—Green School. He restores painting to be a natural behavior without professional restrictions, and a private expression with less unitarianism.
But I am also Buddhist and Quaker, and have seriously explored Catholicism, Episcopalianism, Judaism, Sufi, Shinto, Islam, Unitarianism, Ethical Culture, and a whole host more of religions.
